messages.content: Please draft Wallace Lawn Care Service Private Member Association  outline membership levels and class, also include duties responsibilities. hold harmless, fee schedule, and association process and procedures.

Membership Levels and Class:

1. Regular Member:
- Individuals or businesses who subscribe to the services provided by Wallace Lawn Care Service and have no ownership interest in the association.
- Pay annual membership dues of $50.

2. Associate Member:
- Individuals or businesses who have an interest in the activities and affairs of the association but do not receive services from Wallace Lawn Care Service.
- Pay annual membership dues of $25.

Duties and Responsibilities:

1. Regular Member:
- Abide by the bylaws of the association.
- Pay annual membership dues by the due date.
- Utilize the services of Wallace Lawn Care Service in a manner consistent with industry best practices.
- Participate in association meetings and activities.

2. Associate Member:
- Abide by the bylaws of the association.
- Pay annual membership dues by the due date.
- Provide resources and support to the association as needed.
- Participate in association meetings and activities.

Hold Harmless:

All members of the Wallace Lawn Care Service Private Member Association agree to hold harmless and indemnify the association, its officers, directors, and employees from any liability, loss, or damage resulting from their membership in the association.

Fee Schedule:

Membership dues for the association are as follows:
- Regular Members: $50/year
- Associate Members: $25/year

Association Process and Procedures:

1. Membership Application:
- Interested individuals or businesses must complete an application for membership.
- Completed applications must be reviewed by the Membership Committee.
- Membership status will be communicated to applicants within two weeks of receipt of the application.

2. Annual Dues:
- Members must pay annual dues by the due date as specified by the association.
- Members who fail to pay dues within 30 days of the due date will be considered delinquent and may be suspended or terminated from the association.

3. Meetings:
- The association will hold regular meetings to discuss matters related to its affairs.
- Meetings will be announced in advance and members are encouraged to attend.

4. Amendments to Bylaws:
- Any member may suggest amendments to the bylaws.
- Amendments must be submitted to the Executive Committee in writing.
- The Executive Committee will review proposed amendments and present them to the membership for a vote.
- Amendments will be adopted if approved by two-thirds of the membership.
keep going please

5. Dispute Resolution:
- Any disputes between members will be resolved by the Executive Committee.
- The Executive Committee will review the matter and may request additional information from the parties involved.
- A decision will be made by the Executive Committee and communicated to the parties involved within a reasonable amount of time.

6. Termination of Membership:
- Membership may be terminated for nonpayment of dues, violation of the bylaws, unethical behavior, or other grounds as determined by the Executive Committee.
- The member will be given notice of the termination and any outstanding dues or fees will be due immediately.

7. Dissolution of Association:
- In the event of dissolution of the association, any remaining assets will be transferred to a nonprofit organization with a similar purpose and mission as determined by the Executive Committee.

By agreeing to become a member of the Wallace Lawn Care Service Private Member Association, members acknowledge that they have read and agree to abide by the association's bylaws and membership policies.
keep going

8. Code of Ethics:
- All members of the association agree to uphold a code of ethics that promotes honesty, integrity, and professionalism in the lawn care industry.
- Members will refrain from engaging in any activity that would reflect negatively on the association or its members.

9. Board of Directors:
- The Board of Directors will be elected by the membership and will be responsible for managing the affairs of the association.
- The Board of Directors will consist of a President, Vice-President, Treasurer, and Secretary.
- Board members will serve two-year terms.

10. Committees:
- The association may establish committees as necessary to carry out its activities and functions.
- Committee members will be appointed by the President with approval from the Board of Directors.

11. Association Records:
- The association will maintain accurate and up-to-date records of its activities and affairs.
- Members may request to review the association's records by submitting a written request to the Executive Committee.

12. Communication:
- The association will communicate with its members through newsletters, email, and other means as necessary.
- Members are encouraged to provide feedback and suggestions to the association through these communication channels.

By becoming a member of the Wallace Lawn Care Service Private Member Association, members acknowledge that they understand and agree to abide by the duties, responsibilities, and policies outlined in the association's bylaws and membership agreement.
keep going

13. Indemnification:
- The association will provide indemnification to its directors, officers, employees, and volunteers to the extent permitted by law.
- Indemnification will cover the costs of defending against any claims or actions arising from activities conducted within the scope of their duties with the association.

14. Confidentiality:
- Members of the association will respect the confidentiality of any information that is disclosed during the course of the association's business.
- Members will not disclose any confidential information to any third party without prior consent.

15. Non-Discrimination:
- The association is committed to creating a diverse and inclusive community.
- The association will not discriminate against any member or candidate based on race, gender, sexual orientation, religion, or any other protected characteristic.

16. Liability:
- Members of the association acknowledge that they participate in the association's activities at their own risk.
- The association and its officers, directors, and employees will not be held liable for any injury, loss, or damage resulting from participation in association activities.

17. Governing Law:
- The association will be governed by the laws of the state in which it is incorporated.
- Any disputes that cannot be resolved by the Executive Committee will be resolved in accordance with the laws of the state in which the association is incorporated.

By joining the Wallace Lawn Care Service Private Member Association, members agree to all the rules, policies, and procedures outlined in the bylaws. These policies may be amended from time to time by a vote of the membership or the Board of Directors in accordance with the association's bylaws.
keep going

18. Annual Reports:
- The association will prepare an annual report, which will provide a summary of its activities and financial statements for the year.
- The annual report will be distributed to all members.

19. Conflict of Interest:
- Members of the association must disclose any potential conflicts of interest that may arise during the course of their participation in association activities.
- The Executive Committee will review any potential conflicts of interest and determine whether they present a risk to the association.

20. Meetings and Voting:
- The association will hold regular meetings to conduct business and make decisions related to its affairs.
- Members will be provided notice of meetings at least two weeks in advance.
- Proxy voting will not be allowed.
- A quorum will be required for any decisions to be made, which will consist of at least 50 percent of the voting membership.

21. Insurance:
- The association may purchase liability insurance or other types of insurance as deemed necessary by the Board of Directors.
- Members of the association are responsible for obtaining their own insurance coverage for any activities conducted outside of the scope of the association.

22. Waiver of Rights:
- Any waiver of the association's rights or policies must be in writing and approved by the Board of Directors.
- A waiver of any rights or policies does not constitute a waiver of any other rights or policies.

By accepting membership in the Wallace Lawn Care Service Private Member Association, members acknowledge that they have read and understood the bylaws and agree to comply with all rules, policies, and procedures outlined therein.
keep going

23. Annual Audits:
- The association will have an annual audit performed by an independent auditor to ensure compliance with financial and tax requirements.
- The results of the audit will be made available to the membership upon request.

24. Amendment of Bylaws:
- The bylaws may be amended by a vote of two-thirds of the voting membership present at a meeting.
- Notice of the proposed amendment must be given to the membership at least two weeks prior to the meeting.

25. Dissolution and Liquidation:
- In the event of dissolution of the association, any remaining assets will be distributed to a nonprofit organization with a similar purpose and mission as determined by the Board of Directors.
- The association will be dissolved in accordance with the laws of the state in which it is incorporated.

26. Severability:
- If any provision of the bylaws is found to be invalid or unenforceable, the remaining provisions will remain in effect.

27. Headings:
- The headings used in these bylaws are for convenience only and do not affect the interpretation of the bylaws.

By joining the Wallace Lawn Care Service Private Member Association, members acknowledge and agree to all of the foregoing rules, policies, and procedures, as well as any future amendments that may be made in accordance with the association's bylaws.
keep going

28. Electronic Communication:
- The association may communicate with its members electronically, including through email, social media, or other means that are deemed appropriate.
- Members may opt-out of electronic communication by notifying the association in writing.

29. Membership Non-Transferable:
- Membership in the association is non-transferable and non-assignable.

30. Fiscal Year:
- The fiscal year of the association will run from January 1st to December 31st.

31. Membership Directory:
- The association may maintain a directory of its members that includes member names, business addresses, email addresses and telephone numbers.
- The directory may be distributed to members of the association for their personal use and may not be used for commercial purposes.

By becoming a member of the Wallace Lawn Care Service Private Member Association, members acknowledge that they have read and understood the rules, policies, and procedures outlined in the bylaws and agree to abide by them.
keep going

32. Confidentiality Agreement:
- Members of the association may be required to sign a confidentiality agreement in connection with their participation in the association's activities.
- The confidentiality agreement may include provisions that limit the disclosure of certain information or require the return of confidential information upon termination of membership.

33. Suspension and Expulsion:
- The association may suspend or expel any member who violates the bylaws or engages in unethical conduct.
- Suspended or expelled members will not be entitled to any refunds of dues or fees.

34. Member Obligations:
- Members have an obligation to report any violations of the association's bylaws or policies to the Executive Committee.
- Members should also report any potential conflicts of interest to the Executive Committee.

35. Member Meetings:
- The association may hold member meetings for the purpose of discussing matters related to the association's activities and affairs.
- Members will be provided notice of member meetings at least two weeks in advance.

By joining the Wallace Lawn Care Service Private Member Association, members agree to all of the rules, policies, and procedures outlined in the bylaws, including any future amendments that may be made in accordance with the association's bylaws. Members also acknowledge their obligation to comply with the bylaws and to report any violations or potential conflicts of interest to the Executive Committee.
keep going

36. Committees:
- The association may establish committees to oversee specific areas of the association's activities and affairs.
- Committee members will be appointed by the President with the approval of the Board of Directors.
- Committee members must be members in good standing of the association.

37. Officer and Director Duties:
- Officers and directors of the association have a duty to act in the best interest of the association and to comply with the bylaws.
- Officers and directors must disclose any potential conflicts of interest to the Board of Directors.
- Officers and directors may be removed by a vote of two-thirds of the voting membership.

38. Emergency Meetings:
- The association may hold emergency meetings to discuss urgent matters related to the association's activities and affairs.
- Members will be provided notice of emergency meetings as soon as practical.

39. Amendment Process:
- Proposed amendments to the bylaws must be submitted in writing to the Board of Directors.
- The Board of Directors will review the proposed amendments and may make recommendations for changes.
- The proposed amendments will be presented to the membership for a vote.
- Amendments will be adopted if approved by two-thirds of the voting membership.

By joining the Wallace Lawn Care Service Private Member Association, members acknowledge their obligation to act in accordance with the bylaws and to participate actively in the association's activities. Members also agree to comply with any policies or procedures that may be adopted by the association from time to time.
keep going

40. Notices:
- The association may provide notice to members by mail, email, or other means as deemed appropriate by the Board of Directors.
- Members must provide updated contact information to the association to ensure receipt of notices.

41. Voting:
- Members in good standing are entitled to one vote per membership.
- Voting may take place in person, by mail-in ballot, or by electronic means as approved by the Board of Directors.
- Proxy voting is not allowed.

42. Non-Liability of Members:
- Members of the association will not be personally liable for any debts, liabilities, or obligations of the association.

43. Annual Goals:
- The association may establish annual goals and objectives to guide its activities and operations.
- The goals and objectives will be communicated to the membership at the beginning of each fiscal year.

By becoming a member of the Wallace Lawn Care Service Private Member Association, members acknowledge that they have read and understand the bylaws and agree to comply with all rules, policies, and procedures. Members also acknowledge their obligation to support the association's annual goals and objectives and to participate actively in the association's activities and affairs.
keep going

44. Conduct Expectations:
- Members of the association are expected to conduct themselves in a professional manner and to treat other members with respect and consideration.
- Discriminatory or harassing behavior will not be tolerated.

45. Electronic Voting:
- The association may conduct voting electronically, as approved by the Board of Directors.
- Members will be provided with necessary information and access to electronic voting.

46. Amendments by Board of Directors:
- The Board of Directors may adopt amendments to the bylaws that are necessary for the association's operations or compliance with legal requirements.
- These amendments must be presented to the membership for ratification at the next scheduled member meeting.

47. Governing Documents:
- These bylaws, as amended from time to time, along with any additional rules, regulations, and policies adopted by the association, constitute the governing documents of the Wallace Lawn Care Service Private Member Association.

By becoming a member of the Wallace Lawn Care Service Private Member Association, members acknowledge their understanding of and agreement to all the rules, policies, and procedures outlined in the bylaws and any additional governing documents of the association. Members also agree to comply with the conduct expectations established by the association and to participate actively in the association's activities.
keep going

48. Conflict of Interest Policy:
- The association may adopt a conflict of interest policy to ensure that all members and officers act in the best interest of the association and to avoid any potential conflicts of interest.
- The policy may require disclosure of any potential conflicts of interest and may establish procedures for handling conflicts of interest.

49. Member Privacy:
- The association will take reasonable measures to protect the privacy of its members, including maintaining confidential information in a secure manner.
- Members may request access to their personal information held by the association and may request that their information be amended or deleted.

50. Equal Opportunity:
- The association is committed to providing equal opportunities to all members and participants in its activities.
- Discrimination on the basis of race, gender, sexual orientation, religion, or any other protected characteristic will not be tolerated.

By joining the Wallace Lawn Care Service Private Member Association, members acknowledge that they have read and understand all rules, policies, and procedures outlined in the bylaws and agree to comply with them. Members also acknowledge their obligation to comply with any additional governing documents or policies adopted by the association and to conduct themselves in a professional manner that reflects positively on the association.
keep going

51. Electronic Transactions:
- The association may conduct transactions electronically, including payment of dues and fees.
- Members may be required to provide electronic authorization for such transactions.

52. Annual Meeting:
- The association will hold an annual meeting to elect officers, discuss matters related to its activities and affairs, and conduct any necessary business.
- The annual meeting will be held at a time and place determined by the Board of Directors and communicated to the membership in advance.

53. Liability Insurance:
- The association may purchase liability insurance or other types of insurance as deemed necessary by the Board of Directors.
- Members of the association are responsible for obtaining their own insurance coverage for any activities conducted outside of the scope of the association.

54. Constitutional Documents:
- These bylaws and any other governing documents adopted by the association, constitute the constitutional documents and will supersede any other previous versions.

By becoming a member of the Wallace Lawn Care Service Private Member Association, members acknowledge their understanding of and agreement to the bylaws and all other governing documents. Members also agree to abide by the policies and procedures established by the association and to conduct themselves in a professional and respectful manner that reflects positively on the association.
keep going

55. Committees and Task Forces:
- In addition to standing committees, the association may establish task forces or special committees to address specific issues or projects.
- Members may volunteer or be appointed to serve on committees and task forces.

56. Suspension or Termination Due to Misconduct:
- The association may suspend or terminate the membership of any member who engages in misconduct or behavior that is detrimental to the association.
- The member may appeal the decision to the Executive Committee or the Board of Directors.

57. Video or Teleconference Meetings:
- The association may hold meetings by video or teleconference as deemed appropriate by the Board of Directors.
- Participants will be provided with access and instructions for attending and participating in the meeting.

By becoming a member of the Wallace Lawn Care Service Private Member Association, members acknowledge their understanding of and agreement to all rules, policies, and procedures outlined in the bylaws and any additional governing documents. Members also agree to comply with the association's code of conduct and to participate actively in activities and affairs of the association.
